mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, directly lifting S&P 500 valuations and producing positive forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in declining rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate reading above 4.5% signals deteriorating labor demand and triggers risk-off positioning, depressing S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE exceeds 4.5% in a monthly release
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in industrial production and a decline in real retail sales indicate a synchronized demand-supply mismatch that compresses corporate margins, leading to negative equity returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint decline of INDPRO and RSAFS does not precede negative 60-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: During active federal funds rate hikes, a month-over-month increase in CPIAUCSL accelerates inflation expectations, forcing the market to price in prolonged higher rates and depressing equity valuations over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly negative when both CPI and the funds rate rise simultaneously, or the interaction effect vanishes in post-2008 data.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust economic activity, supporting corporate earnings growth and driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: Positive payroll growth months do not yield statistically higher 5-day forward returns than negative payroll months, or predictive rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in housing starts below 1.2 million units signals weakening consumer confidence and credit availability, triggering a risk-off rotation that reduces S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 1.2 million units in a monthly release
failure_condition: Housing starts below the threshold do not precede negative 20-day SPX returns more than 50% of the time, or the signal fails in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production rises while real GDP growth decelerates, it indicates a supply glut that forces price cuts and margin compression, leading to negative equity returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The divergence between INDPRO and GDPC1 does not predict negative 60-day forward returns with greater than 55% accuracy, or the interaction term loses significance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-interest-rate environment, a rising unemployment rate acts as a recession warning, causing equity markets to price in earnings contractions and depressing returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rising unemployment does not lead to negative 60-day SPX returns when rates exceed 4.0%, or the regime-specific signal shows no incremental predictive power over baseline models.
